title: sv.wire and sv.reg should get merged
body:
rtl.wire and sv.reg serve the same purpose, to declare a named value.  However, they do so with different data types.  It's weird that
a) variable declarations get an op per type.  Reg and Wire are *types* in verilog.
b) There is no way to use any other primitive type in port or struct declarations.  We default to implicit wire, but reg and logic are also reasonable things to want.
c) There is no way to declare a variable of logic type.
d) standard types can map to multiple verilog types depending on the operation used.  Seems strange that type lowering is determined by operation in some contexts and not others.
